records.ety: [OF. estor, estore, built, erected, p. p. of estorer to build, restore, to store. See Store, v. t.]

defs: A set of rooms on the same floor or level; a floor, or the space between two floors. Also, a horizontal division of a building's exterior considered architecturally, which need not correspond exactly with the stories within.
notes: ☞ A story comprehends the distance from one floor to another; as, a story of nine or ten feet elevation. The spaces between floors are numbered in order, from below upward; as, the lower, second, or third story; a house of one story, of two stories, of five stories.

records.ety: [OE. storie, OF. estoire, F. histoire, fr. L. historia. See History.]

defs: A narration or recital of that which has occurred; a description of past events; a history; a statement; a record.
quotes: One malcontent who did indeed get a name in story.
Venice, with its unique city and its Impressive story.
The four great monarchies make the subject of ancient story.

defs: The relation of an incident or minor event; a short narrative; a tale; especially, a fictitious narrative less elaborate than a novel; a short romance.
A euphemism or child's word for “a lie;” a fib; as, to tell a story.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Storied (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Storying.]

defs: To tell in historical relation; to make the subject of a story; to narrate or describe in story.
quotes: How worthy he is I will leave to appear hereafter, rather than story him in his own hearing.
It is storied of the brazen colossus in Rhodes, that it was seventy cubits high.
